user_message: Just came out of QBR preparation with Woodgrove Labs. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 08:20 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-09 12:47 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 11:24 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-M trap (No native 'champion-change motion' in skill set); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-M failure mode (No native 'champion-change motion' in skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-M -- No native 'champion-change motion' in skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
